channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: South Korea will hold a presidential election June 3 to choose Yoon's successor
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea will hold a snap presidential election June 3 to choose Yoon Suk Yeol's successor after the conservative was ousted over his imposition of martial law late last year.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A US-Russian crew of 3 launches to the International Space Station
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — NASA astronaut Jonny Kim and two Russian crewmates launched successfully to the International Space Station on Tuesday on board a Russian spacecraft.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Springtime is when Germany indulges its passion for white asparagus
item.description: BEELITZ, Germany (AP) — Asparagus season has arrived in Germany, signaling the unofficial beginning of springtime. There is no other vegetable that Germans obsess about as much as white asparagus.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Prince Harry appeals the loss of his UK security detail
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Harry wants his British security detail restored and is taking his case to an appeals court.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump's DHS revokes legal status for migrants who entered the US on Biden-era CBP One app
item.description: MCALLEN, Texas (AP) — Migrants who were temporarily allowed to live in the United States by using a Biden-era online appointment app have been told to leave the country “immediately,” officials said Monday. It was unclear how many beneficiaries would be affected.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Clem Burke, multifaceted drummer of iconic rock group Blondie, has died
item.description: Clem Burke, whose versatile drumming propelled the iconic rock group Blondie during its decades of music that spanned from new-wave punk to disco-infused tunes, has died.
item.dc:creator: Hallie Golden, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How Philadelphia police draw on personal experiences to respond to mental health crises
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — On a frigid February morning, Vanity Cordero, a Philadelphia police officer, heard a call over the radio for a man threatening to jump from a bridge. The details sounded familiar.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Robert Caro, Salman Rushdie and Sandra Cisneros honored by Authors Guild
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Robert Caro, Salman Rushdie and Sandra Cisneros were honored Monday night at an Authors Guild dinner gala that celebrated the written word and its vital role in the preservation of democracy.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Utah Red Cross volunteers deployed to assist with flooding, tornado relief in several other states
item.description: Volunteers with the American Red Cross of Utah disaster team have been deployed to several states that have been affected by recent tornadoes and flooding in the Midwest and South.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Swollen rivers are flooding towns in the US South after a prolonged deluge of rain
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Days of unrelenting downpours swelled rivers to near record levels across Kentucky on Monday, submerging neighborhoods and threatening a famed bourbon distillery in the state capital.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tennessee repeat felon charged in Rhodes College student's 2021 murder learns his fate
item.description: Memphis, Tennessee, felon Rainess Holmes has been sentenced in connection with Rhodes College student Andrew Rainer's October 2021 murder during a home invasion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Idaho mom who killed 2 of her kids goes on trial over death of her husband
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Lori Vallow Daybell, who was convicted of killing her two youngest children and conspiring to murder a romantic rival, is on trial again Monday. This time, she's accused in Arizona of conspiring to murder her estranged husband.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO and JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: An Israeli strike hit near a charity kitchen in Gaza as Palestinians gathered for food
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli strike on Monday hit next to a charity kitchen where Palestinians crowded to receive cooked meals as food supplies dwindle under Israel's monthlong blockade of the Gaza Strip, one of a string of attacks in the territory that killed more than 30 people, mos
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Photos: Step inside Epic Universe, Universal’s newest theme park
item.description: Universal Orlando has revealed the first details of its highly anticipated new theme park, Epic Universe, which will officially open on May 22.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EPA says it will reconsider safety of fluoride in drinking water
item.description: Fluoride is intentionally added to drinking water to prevent tooth decay. About 200 million Americans drink water with added fluoride.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Bridal shops brace for impact as tariffs affecting wedding dress prices
item.description: Most of the world's wedding dresses are made in China and the tariffs are increasing wholesale prices for dresses and fabrics.
item.dc:creator: Ella Wales, NewsNation

item.title: Epic Universe opens a portal to the future of theme parks
item.description: As Universal Orlando Resort gears up for the highly anticipated May 22 opening of Epic Universe, it’s clear the resort is entering a new era - one that could redefine what a theme park can offer families.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, WGN 9

item.title: South Carolina's highest court refuses to stop second firing squad execution
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina's highest court on Monday rejected the last major appeal from Mikal Mahdi, who is scheduled to die by firing squad later this week for the ambush killing of an off-duty police officer.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts pauses order to return Maryland dad mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: Chief Justice John Roberts agreed Monday to pause a midnight deadline for the Trump administration to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maine sues the Trump administration over funding freeze after dispute over transgender athletes
item.description: SCARBOROUGH, Maine (AP) — Maine officials sued the administration of President Donald Trump on Monday to try to stop the government from freezing federal money in the wake of a dispute over transgender athletes in sports.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FCC asks for public input on local broadcast regulations
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) is asking the public to help rethink its regulation of local broadcasters. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr announced the "Delete, delete, delete" initiative last month, urging citizens to suggest which regulations should be revised or eliminated.
item.dc:creator: KTLA 5 News, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration asks Supreme Court to block return of man wrongly deported to El Salvador
item.description: The Trump administration on Monday asked the Supreme Court to block a court order requiring it to return a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador. The Justice Department argued in an emergency appeal to the justices that U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Husband of Dem mayor arrested for DUI while driving toddler son hits back at critics
item.description: After the Democrat mayor of Lumberton Township, New Jersey, was arrested for DUI, her husband, Jason Carty, wants critics to back off.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service (NPS) has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, ABC 4

item.title: Senator says he was joking about using violence against journalists
item.description: Sen. Markwayne Mullin, R-Okla., suggested that a lot "less false stories" would be written by journalists if they got into physical altercations with politicians.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Judge dismisses former Mississippi governor's defamation lawsuit against news outlet
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (AP) — A judge has dismissed a lawsuit by former Mississippi Gov. Phil Bryant that claimed a local news outlet defamed him in public comments about its Pulitzer Prize-winning reporting on the misspending of $77 million in federal welfare funds.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mega Millions tickets rise to $5, but the lottery promises more giant jackpots
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Mega Millions players will get slightly better odds and should start seeing more billion-dollar jackpots, but at a cost — literally — with tickets for the multistate lottery jumping in price to $5.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Jr. visits epicenter of Texas measles outbreak after death of second child who was infected
item.description: U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. visited the epicenter of Texas' still-growing measles outbreak on Sunday, the same day a funeral was held for a second young child who was not vaccinated and died from a measles-related illness.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: No state has axed its income tax on wages in 45 years. Now 2 Southern states are on a path to do so
item.description: About 45 years have passed since a U.S. state last eliminated its income tax on wages and salaries. But with recent actions in Mississippi and Kentucky, two states now are on a path to do so, if their economies keep growing.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 22,600 lbs of bratwurst recalled in 10 states over possible plastic contamination
item.description: TOPEKA (KSNT) — Federal health officials say thousands of pounds of bratwurst sold in 10 states are being recalled because the sausages may contain hard plastic. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Self, ABC 4

item.title: Researchers developing at-home smell test to detect Alzheimer’s
item.description: A smell test is less invasive and more accessible than other methods like PET scans, lumbar punctures or blood tests.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: California town reports third fatality related to rare virus linked to death of Gene Hackman's wife
item.description: Investigators recently blamed the rare virus for the death of Betsy Arakawa, the wife of actor Gene Hackman.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Jaguar and Land Rover carmaker pauses shipments to US over Trump tariffs
item.description: The company that makes Jaguar and Land Rover vehicles said on Saturday it would pause shipments to the U.S., days after President Donald Trump’s tariffs took effect.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Energy Department considers more than 40 percent of its staff non-essential as layoffs loom
item.description: The Energy Department's portfolio includes both energy technology and innovation as well as nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5
